topic: Google Antigravity vs Cursor vs Claude Code 2026: Agent-First IDE Compared
keyword: google antigravity vs cursor vs claude code

key_points: Google Antigravity is free in public preview with rate limits
Cursor remains the most polished daily driver IDE
Claude Code is best for terminal-first agentic workflows
Antigravity lacks MCP support which Cursor and Claude Code have
Antigravity builds features in 42s vs 68s for Cursor but trails on Terminal-Bench
Antigravity introduces Manager View for orchestrating up to 5 parallel agents
Cursor integrates AI into familiar VS Code environment with developer control
Claude Code operates terminal-first with multi-step autonomous task planning
Era has shifted from 'which AI writes better code' to 'which AI workforce to manage'
Hybrid approach of Cursor + Claude Code is most common among experienced developers
Cursor: refined daily driver with deep VS Code integration and background cloud agents
Claude Code: high-performance terminal agent with whole-project codebase awareness
Google Antigravity: experimental agent-first IDE from Google, built around Gemini 3
Claude Code uses 5.5x fewer tokens than Cursor for identical tasks
Antigravity's stability issues and rate limit problems make it risky for production work
Antigravity: agent-first development focused on delegating tasks to autonomous agents
Claude Code: terminal-first control for developers who prefer staying in the command line
Antigravity generates Artifacts (task lists, screenshots, browser recordings) for auditability
Claude Code excels on Terminal-Bench at 54.2% vs Antigravity's lower score
Antigravity supports multi-model: Gemini 3.1 Pro, Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-OSS-120B
Cursor Teams: $40/user/month with background cloud agents and CLI launched Jan 2026
Claude Code: $20/mo Pro, $100-200/mo Max (5x usage), $125/seat enterprise
Google Antigravity: Free preview, Pro $20/mo, Ultra $249.99/mo
Antigravity MCP support added in early 2026 but limited (no OAuth specs)
Claude Code Pro users get ~44,000 tokens per 5-hour period

Cursor Teams costs $40/user/month vs Claude Code enterprise at $125/user/month — 3x cost difference at scale

Claude Code gains /ultrareview command in 2026 for multi-pass bug detection with deeper analysis than standard review
